elements.caption: Meeting Agenda Topics and Presenters
elements.summary: Table listing the agenda topics, tab numbers, and presenters for the February 17, 2022 meeting.

sections.title: North Carolina 911 Board Education Committee Meeting Draft- Minutes

elements.text:
North Carolina 911 Board
Education Committee Meeting
Draft- Minutes

content.text: Virtual – Meeting was conducted using simultaneous communication.

elements.caption: Meeting Attendees - September 23, 2021
elements.summary: Table listing committee members, staff, and guests attending the meeting, indicating phone or in-person attendance.

content.text:
Mr. Greene kicked off the meeting by welcoming the committee and guests. Chief 
Tommy Cole was introduced as the newest committee member; he expressed he was looking forward to serving 
on the committee.
Ms. Harris welcomed committee members and guests.
Mr. Greene asked Ms. Turbeville to conduct the roll call.

Ms. Wright made the motion to approve the minutes and Ms. Anderson made a 
motion to second the minutes. All attending committee members voted, and the motion carried unanimously.
The roster of roll call votes for action items for this meeting included below as part of these minutes.

Mr. Bradford gave a brief report and noted the only Bill pending that specifically relates to 
the Board, is HB404. He reminded the Board this Bill was referred to conference committee. The Bill came out of 
committee with some new language with substantive changes affecting providers and has been referred to 
committee. The Budget Bill is going through the negotiation process. At this point, it includes a great deal of 
change and opportunities that relate to Broadband. These do not directly impact the Board however these 
changes will influence local government and will have a positive impact overall in terms of the ESInet and 
communications which do concern the Board. There are also several new federal government opportunities that 
will positively impact local government. Mr. Bradford encourages monitoring these negotiations and 
opportunities. The FCC Strikeforce final report was presented to Congress on Thursday, September 23, and it has 
been published on the FCC’s website. This will be sent out to the Board next week and Ms. Harris and Mr. 
Bradford are planning an in-depth discussion with staff to present these changes. Mr. Bradford will also provide 
a more direct report about the content and expectations going forward regarding the new FCC Rules and 
Strikeforce during the October Board meeting.

elements.alt: Google Analytics dashboard showing Webpage Summary with a bar chart for Which channels are driving engagement? (Organic Search is highest) and a table for Top Referral Sites.

sections.title: Website Analytics: Top 20 Cities out of the 453 Sessions

elements.alt: Dashboard showing Top Cities by Sessions (Durham, Charlotte, Raleigh are top 3) and a pie chart for New Visitor vs Returning (74.6% Returning, 25.4% New Visitor).

sections.title: Webpage Analytics: Top 10 Clicks after accessing the Recruitment Webpage

elements.alt: Dashboard showing Top Clicks URL list, with durhamnc.gov as the top click with 200 total.

sections.title: URLs Accessed from the Recruitment Webpage

content.text: Between 09/06/2021 - 11/07/2021 there were 499 users to the site and 453 sessions.
Organic Search drove 57.8% of the overall sessions during this time
There is only Google Data Studio data available beginning 08/24/2021
Looking at traffic before the campaign began (08/24/2021 – 09/05/2021) there were 27 users equaling 21 sessions.
Organic searching during this time drove 33.3% of the overall sessions during this time.
The cable campaign helped increase organic search traffic by
Applications Received during the Campaign (2 months)
Monthly Percentage Increase during the Campaign
Shared video on social media received multiple likes and shares
Recruitment ended 5 days after campaign began; 305 hits on social media
Invited to a job fair in January as a result of the PSA
No recruitment during campaign; Did receive job inquiries
Shared video on social media -immediately shared and numerous public comments
Received some applications out of the normal area

elements.caption: PSAP Impacts – Application Intake
elements.summary: Table showing PSAP application intake statistics before and during the campaign.

sections.title: ROI – Effectiveness of the Pilot Program

content.text: Increase in Number of Applications – Most PSAPs in the pilot program who were actively recruiting saw an increase in applications.
Social Media Impacts – PSAPs reporting multiple hits, shares and likes
Website Analytics – Reporting high traffic with the source coming from direct and organic search.
Direct – Interested party directly went to the site by typing the URL.
Organic Search – PSA pushed interested party to website to find out more information.
Increased Awareness of Job Opportunity – PSAP invited to job fair.
